SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.io

Interface IFileDefinitionHandler

All Known Implementing Classes:
JobFileDefinitionHandler
public interface IFileDefinitionHandler
This interface defines methods for handling and managing file descriptors (DD) associated with job steps in a system. It provides functionalities for resolving, adding, removing, and listing DD entries, as well as handling different disposition states.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addDd(String ddname, Dd dd)
    Adds a DD (Data Definition) to the collection associated with a job step.
    Dd
    getDd(String ddname)
    Retrieves the Dd object associated with the specified data definition name (DDNAME).
    void
    handleDispAbnormal()
    Handles the disposition status when an abnormal completion of a job step occurs.
    void
    handleDispNormal()
    Handles the disposition status when the normal completion of a job step occurs.
    void
    handleDispStatus()
    Handles the disposition status of all defined data definitions (DDs) associated with the current job step.
    List<Dd>
    list()
    Retrieves a list of all Dd objects associated with the current job step.
    void
    removeDd(String ddname)
    Removes a data definition (DD) entry associated with the specified DD name (DDNAME).
    FileDescriptor
    resolve(Dd dd)
    Resolves the given Dd object to a corresponding FileDescriptor.
    FileDescriptor
    resolve(String ddName)
    Resolves a file descriptor based on the given data definition name (DDNAME).

  • Method Details

    • resolve

      FileDescriptor resolve(String ddName)
      Resolves a file descriptor based on the given data definition name (DDNAME). This method is responsible for mapping a DDNAME to its corresponding FileDescriptor, enabling further processing or access to the associated resource.
      Parameters:
      ddName - the name of the data definition (DDNAME) to be resolved
      Returns:
      the FileDescriptor associated with the provided DDNAME, or null if no matching descriptor is found

    • resolve

      FileDescriptor resolve(Dd dd) throws GdgException
      Resolves the given Dd object to a corresponding FileDescriptor.
      Parameters:
      dd - the Dd object representing the data definition to be resolved
      Returns:
      the resolved FileDescriptor corresponding to the provided Dd object
      Throws:
      GdgException - if an error occurs during the resolution process

    • addDd

      void addDd(String ddname, Dd dd)
      Adds a DD (Data Definition) to the collection associated with a job step.
      Parameters:
      ddname - the name of the Data Definition (DDNAME) to be added
      dd - the Dd object representing the Data Definition to be added

    • removeDd

      void removeDd(String ddname)
      Removes a data definition (DD) entry associated with the specified DD name (DDNAME). This method deletes the DD entry from the collection of defined job step DDs, ensuring it is no longer available for subsequent operations.
      Parameters:
      ddname - the name of the Data Definition (DDNAME) to be removed

    • list

      List<Dd> list()
      Retrieves a list of all Dd objects associated with the current job step.
      Returns:
      a list of Dd objects representing the data definitions (DDs) currently registered

    • handleDispStatus

      void handleDispStatus() throws DdDispositionException
      Handles the disposition status of all defined data definitions (DDs) associated with the current job step. This method evaluates the disposition statuses and performs necessary actions such as cleanup, validation, or updates to ensure proper management of resources at the conclusion of processing.
      Throws:
      DdDispositionException - if an error occurs during the handling of a disposition status, including when a specific DD cannot be processed or resolved.

    • handleDispNormal

      void handleDispNormal() throws DdDispositionException
      Handles the disposition status when the normal completion of a job step occurs. This method evaluates the disposition of all data definitions (DDs) that are associated with the job step. The process may involve cleanup tasks, resource deallocation, or writing final updates to ensure proper handling of resources and maintain system consistency.
      Throws:
      DdDispositionException - if an error occurs during the processing of the disposition status of any DD, such as when a DD cannot be resolved or processed appropriately.

    • handleDispAbnormal

      void handleDispAbnormal() throws DdDispositionException
      Handles the disposition status when an abnormal completion of a job step occurs. This method evaluates the disposition of all data definitions (DDs) associated with the job step in the event of an error or unexpected termination. Possible actions may include resource cleanup, error handling, or status adjustments to ensure proper system recovery and maintain consistency.
      Throws:
      DdDispositionException - if an error occurs during the processing of the disposition status of any DD, such as when a DD cannot be resolved or processed properly in the abnormal termination scenario.

    • getDd

      Dd getDd(String ddname)
      Retrieves the Dd object associated with the specified data definition name (DDNAME). This method allows access to a previously defined Dd object using its corresponding name.
      Parameters:
      ddname - the name of the data definition (DDNAME) to retrieve
      Returns:
      the Dd object associated with the provided DDNAME, or null if no matching entry is found
